configuration.name: Ariane 64

manufacturer.name: Arianespace
manufacturer.description: Arianespace SA is a multinational company founded in 1980 as the world's first commercial launch service provider. It undertakes the production, operation, and marketing of the Ariane programme. Their vehicles launch exclusively from French Guiana in South America.

manufacturer.name: ArianeGroup
manufacturer.description: ArianeGroup (formerly Airbus Safran Launchers) is an aerospace company based in France. A joint venture between Airbus and Safran, the company was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Issy-les-Moulineaux. It consists of three core arms: aerospace, defence and security.

configuration.description: Ariane 6 variant with four solid boosters, enabling heavier payloads.

mission.name: MTG-S2
mission.description: Fifth of EUMETSAT's third generation of weather satellite.

agencies.name: Arianespace
agencies.description: Arianespace SA is a multinational company founded in 1980 as the world's first commercial launch service provider. It undertakes the production, operation, and marketing of the Ariane programme. Their vehicles launch exclusively from French Guiana in South America.

pad.name: Ariane Launch Area 4

image.name: ELA-4
image.credit: ESA/CNES/Arianespace/Optique Vidéo du CSG - P. PIRON

pad.description: ELA-4, is a launch pad and associated facilities at the Centre Spatial Guyanais in French Guiana. The complex is composed of a launch pad with mobile gantry, an horizontal assembly building and a dedicated launch operations building. ELA-4 is operated by Arianespace as part of the Ariane 6 program.

location.name: Guiana Space Centre, French Guiana

celestial_body.name: Earth
celestial_body.description: Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only astronomical object known to harbor life.

location.description: The Guiana Space Centre is a European spaceport to the northwest of Kourou in French Guiana, a region of France in South America. Kourou is located at a latitude of 5°. In operation since 1968, it is a suitable location for a spaceport because of its equatorial location and open sea to the east.

image.name: Vega & Ariane 5 pads at the Guiana Space Centre

mission.name: Laser Interferometer Space Antenna (LISA)
mission.description: Heliocentric constellation of three laser-equipped spacecraft forming an equilateral triangle with a side distance of 2.5 million km. Its goal is to detect and study gravitational waves.

agencies.name: European Space Agency
agencies.description:
The European Space Agency is an intergovernmental organisation of 22 member states. Established in 1975 and headquartered in Paris, France, ESA has a worldwide staff of about 2,000 employees.

ESA's space flight programme includes human spaceflight (mainly through participation in the International Space Station program); the launch and operation of unmanned exploration missions to other planets and the Moon; Earth observation, science and telecommunication; designing launch vehicles; and maintaining a major spaceport, the Guiana Space Centre at Kourou, French Guiana.

image.name: ESA patch floating in the ISS Cupola

agencies.name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
agencies.description: The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is an independent agency of the executive branch of the United States federal government responsible for the civilian space program, as well as aeronautics and aerospace research. NASA have many launch facilities but most are inactive. The most commonly used pad will be LC-39B at Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
